data.title: NatWest reports Q3 results
data.description:
• NatWest press release (NWG): Q3 Total income of £326M (+14.8% Y/Y).
  • Total assets and liabilities increased by £6.1 billion and £6.2 billion to £189.3 billion and £182.4 billion respectively at 30 September 2025, compared with 31 December 2024.
  • NWM Plc's leverage ratio decreased to 4.5% at 30 September 2025 compared with 5.5% at 31 December 2024, driven by the decrease in Tier 1 capital and higher leverage exposure reflecting increases in trading assets and other financial assets.

data.title: Eni raises 2025 share buy-back by €0.3B to €1.8B
data.description:
• Eni (NYSE:E) on Friday raised its 2025 share buyback commitment by €0.3 billion to €1.8 billion, supported by strategic progress and an improved FY'25 CFFO outlook.
  • Eni increased the group’s expected CFFO before working capital adjustments to €12 billion from the previous €11.5 billion.

data.title: Blackstone to invest $705M in India’s Federal Bank, becoming its largest shareholder
data.description:
• Blackstone (NYSE:BX) will invest about $705 million in India's Federal Bank for a 9.9% stake, the private lender said on Friday.
  • The deal will make the private equity firm the largest shareholder in the bank.

data.title: Disney warns YouTube TV could lose ESPN, National Geographic, FX and other channels by month-end
data.description:
Disney (NYSE:DIS) warned that ESPN, ABC, and other major networks could be pulled from YouTube TV by the end of the month if a new agreement is not reached by October 30 11:59 p.m. ET.

The ongoing dispute over new carriage fees, with Disney (NYSE:DIS) demanding higher payments, puts nearly 10 million YouTube TV subscribers at risk of losing access to key Disney-owned networks, including local ABC stations, all ESPN channels, FX, National Geographic, Disney Channel, Disney Jr., Freeform, and more. Disney (NYSE:DIS) started notifying YouTube TV customers of a potential blackout. 

Google's (NASDAQ:GOOGL) YouTube TV cited concerns about increased costs for its customers. “Unfortunately, Disney is proposing costly economic terms that would raise prices on YouTube TV customers and give our customers fewer choices.” a YouTube TV spokesperson said in an official statement.

YouTube TV subscribers pay $72.99 per month currently. Major price hikes follow blackouts because companies negotiate from stronger positions. YouTube TV said it would offer subscribers a $20 credit if Disney’s (DIS) networks remain unavailable for an extended period.

data.title: Chandra Asri to buy Exxon's Singapore retail fuel station network Esso
data.description:
Chandra Asri Pacific has signed a sale and purchase Agreement to acquire ExxonMobil's (NYSE:XOM) Esso-branded retail fuel station network in Singapore through a special purpose vehicle under a wholly-owned subsidiary of the group, the company said on Friday.

Chandra Asri Group will continue to use the Esso brand, continue purchasing branded fuel from ExxonMobil, and maintain all existing customer loyalty points and cards.

data.title: UK's retail sales rise 1.5% Y/Y in September
data.description:
• Retail sales in the UK increased 0.50% in September of 2025 over the previous month.

  • Retail sales rose 1.50% in September of 2025 over the same month in the previous year.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Alaska Airlines extends flight cancellations through Friday
data.description:
• U.S. carrier Alaska Airlines (NYSE:ALK) is still experiencing an IT outage affecting operations that has resulted in cancellations of some of its flights Thursday evening, it said in a  post on X.
  • The IT outage is not a cybersecurity event and is not related to any other events, as per reports.
  • The issue began around 3:30 p.m. on Thursday, with a failure at the airline’s primary data center.
  • The airline also briefly grounded all of its flights in July for about three hours due to an IT outage.

data.title: Asian markets mixed as economic numbers influence market sentiment
data.description:
Asian indexes stocks traded mixed on Friday, with flash PMI estimates from across the globe expected today. On the trade front, the International Monetary Fund ((IMF)) on Friday urged Asian countries to reduce non-tariff barriers and deepen regional trade integration to lower the vulnerability to U.S. tariffs. Gold prices fell to around $4,110 per ounce, on track to end its nine-week winning streak.

Japan (NKY:IND) gained 1.48%, after key economic numbers were released.

The Japanese yen weakens despite inflation accelerating. Japan’s annual inflation rate rose to 2.9% in September 2025 from August’s 10-month low of 2.7%. 

The S&P Global Japan Manufacturing PMI declined to 48.3 in October 2025, from September’s six-month low of 48.5 and below market forecasts of 48.6, preliminary estimates showed.

China (SHCOMP) +0.22%, with the former reaching fresh ten-year highs after the White House confirmed the Trump-Xi meeting next Thursday on the sidelines of the APEC summit. Trump said he aims to extend the pause on higher tariffs on Chinese goods in exchange for concessions on soybean purchases, fentanyl controls, and rare earth exports, among others. The offshore yuan held steady around 7.12 per dollar.

On the other hand, China’s Communist Party on Thursday outlined key priorities for its upcoming Five-Year Plan, following a four-day closed-door meeting in Beijing, known as the fourth plenum.

Hong Kong (HSI) +0.59% extending gains from the prior session amid broad gains across sectors. Sentiment strengthened after the White House confirmed that U.S. President Trump will meet Chinese leader Xi Jinping on October 30.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.35%. 

Australia (AS51) fell 0.15%, trading in a tight range as investors braced for the upcoming key quarterly inflation figures that could influence the Reserve Bank of Australia's policy outlook.

Australia's flash PMI estimates show drop in factory activity

In the U.S. on Thursday, Wall Street finished higher as investors assessed Tesla's (TSLA) earnings and received news that President Trump will meet with President Xi next Thursday.

U.S. stock futures traded higher: Dow (INDU) +0.16%; S&P 500 (SPX) +0.26%; Nasdaq (US100:IND) +0.39%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Trump cancels trade negotiations with Canada over Reagan ad
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday night he is immediately ending all trade negotiations with Canada because the Ontario provincial government aired an ad featuring former President Ronald Reagan speaking negatively about tariffs.

"Based on their egregious behavior, ALL TRADE NEGOTIATIONS WITH CANADA ARE HEREBY TERMINATED," Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social.

“The Ronald Reagan Foundation has just announced that Canada has fraudulently used an advertisement, which is FAKE, featuring Ronald Reagan speaking negatively about Tariffs,” the post said.

His announcement accused Canada of trying to influence a pending U.S. Supreme Court case that could doom many of his tariffs, including ones on Canada.

Trump imposed a 35% levy on Canadian imports in August, although he has allowed exemptions for goods that fall under the USMCA - a free trade agreement with Mexico and Canada that Trump negotiated during his first term.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
